*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend\HANDOFF.md
@@
 # HANDOFF — Remaining open items
 
 Open items that need continuation context are listed below.
 
 ---
 
+## RFQ Buyer Target edit gate fix — **COMMITTED & PUSHED, PR open** (updated 2026-07-07)
+
+### 1. What this application is
+
+Designflow is POP Creations' product-lifecycle-management web application. The Angular frontend in
+`C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` is used by sourcing, sales, design, licensing, production, and
+factory/vendor users to manage RFQs, item-library records, licensing/production tracking, and related
+workflow screens. This repo is the frontend only; the main PLM API lives in the sibling
+`designflow-backend` service. The RFQ grid is business-critical because it stores vendor prices, buyer
+targets, landed-cost inputs, sell-price calculations, and margin calculations used for quoting retailers.
+
+Current working branch for this repo is `sandbox-albert`, pushed to
+`origin/sandbox-albert`, with PR `sandbox-albert -> develop` open:
+https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141.
+
+### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why
+
+Albert reported that the new RFQ `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` fields were "dead" because users
+could not type into either of them. These fields were part of the 16-column RFQ buyer-margin feature:
+eight editable `"* Buyer Target"` dollar fields and eight calculated `"* Buyer Margin %"` fields. The
+business goal is for permitted users to type a buyer target price on POP/Spruce RFQ rows, then have the
+corresponding buyer margin percentage calculate from the existing RFQ cost basis without altering the
+sourcing team's original sell-price/margin fields.
+
+### 3. Current state
+
+The frontend code fix is committed and pushed:
+
+- Commit: `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`
+- Commit title: `Fix RFQ buyer target edit gating`
+- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
+- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
+- GitHub check verified green: `Forbid Shared DB Bypass`, run
+  https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/actions/runs/28887656252
+
+Files changed:
+
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts`
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.spec.ts`
+
+Behavior now:
+
+- `"Buyer Target"` editability recognizes RFQ rows whose division arrives as:
+  - `Div: "Spruce Lic"`, `Div: "Spruce non-Lic"`, or `Div: "POP Lic"`
+  - `Div: { title: ... }`
+  - `Div: { divCode_id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
+  - `Div: { id: 1 | 8 | 9 }`
+  - lower-case/DB aliases such as `div`, `div_code_fk`, and `rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`
+- POP Lic (`1`), Spruce Lic (`8`), and Spruce non-Lic (`9`) are in buyer-target scope.
+- `"Buyer Margin %"` remains intentionally read-only. It is calculated from the typed Buyer Target by
+  `rfq.component.ts` calling `equation.recalculate_Buyer_Margins(...)`. This is the existing spec in
+  `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md`; do not make buyer-margin cells directly editable unless Albert changes
+  the product requirement.
+
+Local verification passed:
+
+- `yarn jest src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.spec.ts --runInBand`
+  - 12 tests passed.
+- `yarn jest src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.spec.ts --runInBand`
+  - 20 tests passed.
+- `yarn jest src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts --runInBand`
+  - 58 tests passed.
+
+Not yet done:
+
+- PR #141 is still open and has not been merged into `develop`.
+- No sandbox deployment verification was done after this fix inside this session. The fix is frontend-only and pushed,
+  but the live sandbox should be checked after the PR/build/deploy path completes.
+
+Local working tree note:
+
+- There are pre-existing/unrelated local files still present and intentionally not committed:
+  - `src/environments/environment.ts` modified
+  - `proxy.conf.local.json` untracked
+- These were present before this fix and were left alone.
+
+### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work
+
+Initial suspicion was that both `"Buyer Target"` and `"Buyer Margin %"` were editable fields and that the grid might be
+blocking both. Reading `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` and the RFQ grid config showed that this was only partly true:
+`"Buyer Target"` should be editable, but `"Buyer Margin %"` is deliberately read-only because it is a calculated result.
+Changing `"Buyer Margin %"` to editable would have contradicted the shipped feature design.
+
+The first implementation pass hardened the division helper to read `Div`, lower-case `div`,
+`rfqItem_divCode_id_fk`, and `div_code_fk`. The new focused test then failed for
+`isBuyerScopeRow({ rfqItem_divCode_id_fk: 8 })`: the helper found the raw alias value but only treated raw
+`div_code_fk` as a numeric code. The fix was to treat the raw division value itself as the candidate code
+when the value is not an object. After that, the focused helper test passed.
+
+The first `git status` was run from `C:\repos\dflow plm`, which is only the parent folder for the six dflow repos,
+not a git repository. The actual repo for this fix is `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`.
+
+### 5. Root causes and key findings
+
+Root cause: `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` originally checked only
+`data['Div']` and only by matching division names that include `"spruce"` or `"pop"`. Some RFQ grid data paths carry
+division data under aliases or as numeric ids instead of the literal `Div` display field. When AG Grid asked whether
+a Buyer Target cell was editable, `isBuyerScopeRow(params.data)` returned `false`, so AG Grid never opened the numeric
+editor.
+
+Key code locations:
+
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` around the Buyer Target setup:
+  `colHeaders[i].editable = (params) => isBuyerScopeRow(params?.data);`
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` lines 26-63 after the fix:
+  `getDivisionValue(...)`, `getDivisionCodeId(...)`, `isSpruceRow(...)`, `isPopRow(...)`,
+  and `isBuyerScopeRow(...)` now handle the known RFQ shapes.
+- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` defines the buyer columns. The Buyer Margin columns have
+  `editable: false` by design; the Buyer Target columns inherit editability and then get row-level gating in
+  `rfq.component.ts`.
+
+Non-obvious finding: Albert's phrase "can't type anything into either of them" sounds like two editable fields, but
+the intended workflow is "type Buyer Target, read calculated Buyer Margin %." If the UI still feels wrong to Albert,
+clarify whether he now wants Buyer Margin % to be manually overrideable; that is a product change, not a bug fix.
+

+not a git repository. The actual repo for this fix is `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`.
+
+### 5. Root causes and key findings
+
+Root cause: `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` originally checked only
+`data['Div']` and only by matching division names that include `"spruce"` or `"pop"`. Some RFQ grid data paths carry
+division data under aliases or as numeric ids instead of the literal `Div` display field. When AG Grid asked whether
+a Buyer Target cell was editable, `isBuyerScopeRow(params.data)` returned `false`, so AG Grid never opened the numeric
+editor.
+
+Key code locations:
+
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` around the Buyer Target setup:
+  `colHeaders[i].editable = (params) => isBuyerScopeRow(params?.data);`
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/buyer-margin-visibility.ts` lines 26-63 after the fix:
+  `getDivisionValue(...)`, `getDivisionCodeId(...)`, `isSpruceRow(...)`, `isPopRow(...)`,
+  and `isBuyerScopeRow(...)` now handle the known RFQ shapes.
+- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` defines the buyer columns. The Buyer Margin columns have
+  `editable: false` by design; the Buyer Target columns inherit editability and then get row-level gating in
+  `rfq.component.ts`.
+
+Non-obvious finding: Albert's phrase "can't type anything into either of them" sounds like two editable fields, but
+the intended workflow is "type Buyer Target, read calculated Buyer Margin %." If the UI still feels wrong to Albert,
+clarify whether he now wants Buyer Margin % to be manually overrideable; that is a product change, not a bug fix.
+
+### 6. Exact next steps
+
+1. Wait for the PR's normal frontend CI/build path and review.
+   - You'll know it worked when PR #141 shows all required checks green for head SHA
+     `5c3f6dcd642cc336e232ecb454363970ce350446`.
+
+2. After PR #141 is merged/deployed to the sandbox, visually verify the RFQ grid as an admin or a user with the
+   `rfq_buyer_margin` permission toggle.
+   - Open an RFQ with a POP Lic, Spruce Lic, or Spruce non-Lic row.
+   - Find any `"Buyer Target"` column, for example `"Gen FOB Buyer Target"`.
+   - Type a number into the cell and commit the edit.
+   - You'll know it worked when the cell accepts the number and the matching `"Buyer Margin %"` cells recalculate
+     or remain blank according to the existing row/user visibility rules.
+
+3. Confirm the read-only product behavior with Albert if he still expects to type directly into `"Buyer Margin %"`.
+   - You'll know this is resolved when Albert confirms either:
+     - current behavior is correct: Buyer Margin % is calculated only, or
+     - a new requirement exists: Buyer Margin % should be manually editable/overrideable.
+
+4. If a new manual Buyer Margin % requirement is confirmed, implement it as a new product change.
+   - Update `docs/rfq-buyer-margin-plan.md` first.
+   - Change the grid config and RFQ value-setter behavior deliberately.
+   - Add tests proving the new write path persists the manually typed margin and does not get overwritten by Buyer
+     Target recalculation unless that overwrite behavior is explicitly desired.
